name: Chicken Salad Meal Deal Sandwich
popularityContext: Supermarket meal deals (sandwich + snack + drink, typically £3.50-£4) are a defining feature of UK office lunch culture, sold by Tesco, Boots, Sainsbury's and Asda. Chicken salad is consistently one of the best-selling fillings.
prepNotes: Standard pre-packed format: two slices of bread filled with sliced cooked chicken, mayonnaise and salad. Bought ready-made or assembled at home for a packed lunch in under 5 minutes.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods ingredient values; cross-checked against Tesco/Sainsbury's chicken salad sandwich nutrition panels (typically 350-420 kcal, 20-25g protein)

regionAvailability.notes: Available as a pre-packed meal deal item nationwide in supermarket and pharmacy chains.

sources.title: Tesco meal deal chicken sandwich Nutrition
Best Tesco Meal Deals: High-Protein & Low-Calorie

name: Egg Mayo Sandwich (Meal Deal Style)
popularityContext: Egg mayonnaise is one of the longest-running, most consistently stocked sandwich fillings in UK supermarkets and is a Boots/Tesco/Sainsbury's meal deal staple, especially popular with vegetarians.
prepNotes: Hard-boil and chop 2 eggs, mix with mayonnaise, season, and spread between buttered bread with a little salad. Keeps well chilled for a few hours, making it suitable for packed lunches.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods; verified against Boots Simply Egg Mayo Sandwich nutrition panel (449 kcal, 13-15% protein)

regionAvailability.notes: One of the most widely available meal-deal fillings; gluten-free versions also stocked in most large stores.

Calories in Gluten Free Egg Mayo Sandwich by Boots

name: Prawn Mayo Sandwich
popularityContext: A long-standing British sandwich-counter classic, especially popular at Boots, Costa, Sainsbury's and Co-op, often made with brown or granary bread.
prepNotes: Mix cooked prawns with mayonnaise and a squeeze of lemon, season with black pepper, and layer onto bread with shredded lettuce.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods; cross-checked with Sainsbury's and Co-op prawn mayonnaise sandwich nutrition panels (270-335 kcal range)

regionAvailability.notes: Available year-round at most major supermarket and pharmacy meal-deal counters.

sources.title: Calories in Sainsbury's Prawn Mayonnaise Sandwich and Nutrition Facts
Co-op Prawn Mayonnaise Sandwich Nutrition Facts

name: Jacket Potato with Cheese and Beans
popularityContext: Jacket potato (baked potato) with cheese and beans is a classic British café, pub and workplace canteen lunch, sold everywhere from school canteens to motorway services and supermarket cafés.
prepNotes: Pierce the potato and bake at 200C for 60-75 minutes until the skin is crisp and the inside fluffy. Heat the beans separately, split the potato open, top with butter, beans and grated cheese.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for potato, baked beans and cheddar; cross-checked against jacket potato with beans and cheese nutrition panels (520-600 kcal range)

regionAvailability.notes: A café and canteen staple nationwide; potatoes, beans and cheese are budget-friendly and available in every supermarket.

sources.title: Jacket Potato with Beans & Cheese | Don't Go Bacon My Heart
Jacket potato with cheese and beans nutrition

name: Jacket Potato with Tuna Mayo and Sweetcorn
popularityContext: Tuna mayo jacket potato is one of the most ordered baked-potato toppings in UK café and pub menus, valued as a filling, protein-rich lunch option.
prepNotes: Bake the potato at 200C for 60-75 minutes. Flake the drained tuna and mix with mayonnaise, sweetcorn and spring onion, then pile onto the split-open potato.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for potato, tinned tuna and mayonnaise; cross-checked against tuna mayo jacket potato nutrition panels (400-480 kcal range)

regionAvailability.notes: Tinned tuna and potatoes are pantry staples sold in every UK supermarket; this combination is a long-running café menu item.

sources.title: Healthy Baked Potato With Tuna And Cheese
Tuna Mayo Jacket Potato & Salad

name: Leek and Potato Soup with Granary Roll
popularityContext: Leek and potato soup is one of the most enduring British soup recipes, a BBC Good Food and BBC Food staple, commonly served with bread as a simple home or café lunch, especially in colder months.
prepNotes: Soften leeks in butter for 3-4 minutes, add potato and stock, simmer 20 minutes until tender, then blend until smooth and stir through a splash of milk or cream. Serve hot with a granary roll.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for leeks, potato, stock, milk and bread roll, following the standard BBC Food leek and potato soup recipe ratios

regionAvailability.notes: Leeks and potatoes are year-round staples in every UK supermarket; pre-made versions are also sold chilled in most large stores.

sources.title: BBC Food - Leek and potato soup

name: Lentil and Vegetable Soup with Wholemeal Bread
popularityContext: Red lentil soup with carrots and tomatoes is a widely cooked British home lunch, popular for being cheap, filling and freezer-friendly, and a frequent feature on BBC Good Food and NHS healthy-eating recipe pages.
prepNotes: Soften onion and carrot in olive oil, add lentils, tomatoes and stock, then simmer for 25-30 minutes until the lentils break down. Season and serve with a slice of wholemeal bread.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for red lentils, vegetables and bread; lentil-based soups in this style typically provide 14-18g protein and 12-15g fibre per bowl

regionAvailability.notes: Dried lentils and tinned tomatoes are inexpensive pantry items stocked everywhere, making this a common budget lunch.

sources.title: BBC Good Food lentil soup recipes

name: Cheese and Tomato Toastie with Tomato Soup
popularityContext: The combination of a grilled cheese toastie with tomato soup is a long-standing British café and home lunch pairing, widely served in pub kitchens, garden centre cafés and family kitchens alike.
prepNotes: Butter the bread, fill with cheese and tomato slices, then grill or pan-fry in a sandwich press until golden and the cheese melts. Heat the tomato soup separately and serve alongside.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for bread, cheddar and butter, plus a standard tinned tomato soup nutrition panel (typically 100-120 kcal per 300g serving)

regionAvailability.notes: A widely available café combo; tinned tomato soup and cheddar are sold in every UK supermarket.

Classic Grilled Cheese And Tomato Soup

name: Ploughman's Lunch
popularityContext: The ploughman's lunch is a traditional British pub lunch of cold cheese, bread, pickle and salad, dating back to mid-20th-century pub catering and still found on pub menus across the country.
prepNotes: Arrange a thick wedge of cheddar, buttered bread, a spoonful of pickle, a whole or sliced apple, celery, salad and pickled onions on a board. No cooking required — this is an assembled, room-temperature lunch.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for cheddar, bread, pickle, fruit and vegetables in standard pub ploughman's portions

regionAvailability.notes: A pub-menu classic nationwide; all components (cheddar, chutney, crusty bread, pickled onions) are sold in every UK supermarket for a homemade version.

name: Steak and Ale Pie with Mash and Peas
popularityContext: Steak and ale pie is a quintessential British pub lunch, served with mashed potato, peas and gravy in pubs and carveries nationwide, including chains such as Wetherspoons and Sizzling Pubs.

ingredients.item: potato, mashed with butter and milk

prepNotes: Braise diced beef with onion, carrot and ale for 1.5-2 hours until tender, top with pastry and bake until golden. Serve with buttery mashed potato, peas and gravy — a typical sit-down pub portion.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for beef, pastry, potato and vegetables; cross-checked against pub-style steak and ale pie with mash nutrition panels (700-900 kcal for a sit-down portion)

regionAvailability.notes: A pub-lunch classic found nationwide; chilled and frozen steak pies for home cooking are sold in every major supermarket.

name: Scampi and Chips with Tartare Sauce
popularityContext: Scampi and chips is a long-standing British pub and seaside-café lunch staple, made from breaded or battered langoustine/prawn tails, popularised through pub chains and chip shops nationwide.
prepNotes: Deep-fry or oven-bake breaded scampi until golden and cooked through, alongside thick-cut chips. Serve with a spoon of tartare sauce, peas and a lemon wedge, in the classic pub-plate format.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for breaded scampi, fried chips and tartare sauce; pub-style scampi and chips meals typically range 650-800 kcal per plate

regionAvailability.notes: A pub and seaside café staple nationwide; frozen breaded scampi is widely sold for home cooking, particularly at Iceland and Tesco.

name: Scotch Broth with Crusty Bread
popularityContext: Scotch broth is a traditional Scottish soup of lamb, pearl barley and root vegetables, served as a warming lunch throughout Scotland and recognised nationally as a classic British soup.
prepNotes: Simmer lamb with stock for 45 minutes, add pearl barley and root vegetables, then cook for a further 30-40 minutes until the barley and lamb are tender. Serve hot with crusty bread.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for lamb, pearl barley and root vegetables in a standard Scotch broth recipe ratio

regionAvailability.notes: A Scottish national classic with year-round popularity, especially in colder months; lamb, barley and root vegetables are sold in all major supermarkets.

name: Welsh Cawl
popularityContext: Cawl is the national soup-stew of Wales, made with lamb or beef and root vegetables, traditionally eaten as a warming midday meal, often served with a wedge of Welsh cheese and bread.
prepNotes: Brown the lamb, then simmer with stock, potato, swede and carrot for around an hour until tender. Add leeks for the final 10 minutes, then serve hot with a wedge of cheese and bread on the side.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for lamb, root vegetables, Welsh cheese and bread in a standard cawl recipe ratio

regionAvailability.notes: Wales's national dish, widely cooked at home and served in Welsh cafés and pubs; lamb and Caerphilly cheese are stocked in supermarkets across the UK.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala with Rice
popularityContext: Chicken tikka masala is widely regarded as Britain's most popular curry, developed in British Indian restaurants and now a fixture of curry-house and supermarket ready-meal menus, with around 25 million portions eaten annually in the UK.

ingredients.item: tomato-based masala sauce (onion, tomato, cream, spices)

prepNotes: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and spices, grill or pan-sear until charred at the edges, then simmer in a tomato and cream masala sauce for 10-15 minutes. Serve over basmati rice.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for chicken, yoghurt-cream sauce and basmati rice; cross-checked against UK curry house and supermarket ready-meal chicken tikka masala nutrition panels (550-700 kcal with rice)

regionAvailability.notes: Britain's best-selling curry, sold as a ready meal in every supermarket and on virtually every Indian restaurant and takeaway menu nationwide.

name: Chicken Chow Mein
popularityContext: Chicken chow mein is one of the most ordered dishes from British Chinese takeaways, found on virtually every high-street Chinese takeaway menu nationwide as a popular midday lunch order.
prepNotes: Stir-fry sliced chicken in hot oil until cooked through, add vegetables and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, then toss through cooked noodles and soy sauce for a final minute over high heat.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for noodles, chicken and stir-fry vegetables; cross-checked against UK Chinese takeaway chicken chow mein nutrition data (typically 500-600 kcal per portion)

regionAvailability.notes: A nationwide British Chinese takeaway staple; pre-packed stir-fry noodle kits and frozen versions are sold in all major supermarkets.

name: Sweet and Sour Chicken with Egg Fried Rice
popularityContext: Sweet and sour chicken with egg fried rice is a long-standing British Chinese takeaway order, a fixture of high-street takeaway menus since the dish's introduction to the UK in the mid-20th century.

ingredients.item: sweet and sour sauce (tomato, pineapple, vinegar, sugar)

prepNotes: Deep-fry battered chicken pieces until golden, then toss in a simmered sweet and sour sauce with peppers and onion. Serve with egg fried rice, made by stir-frying cooked rice with beaten egg and peas.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for battered chicken, sweet and sour sauce and egg fried rice; cross-checked against UK takeaway sweet and sour chicken with rice nutrition data (650-750 kcal)

regionAvailability.notes: A nationwide British Chinese takeaway classic; frozen and chilled supermarket versions are also widely available.

name: Greggs-Style Sausage Roll with Side Salad
popularityContext: The sausage roll is one of Britain's best-known bakery lunch items, sold by the million weekly at Greggs and independent bakeries, and a fixture of high-street lunch culture nationwide.

ingredients.item: mixed salad leaves, tomato and cucumber

prepNotes: Wrap seasoned sausage meat in puff pastry, brush with egg wash and bake at 200C for 25-30 minutes until golden and crisp. Serve with a simple side salad to balance the meal.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for puff pastry and sausage meat, cross-checked against Greggs sausage roll nutrition panel (328 kcal per 103g roll), plus a side salad with dressing

regionAvailability.notes: Greggs and independent bakeries sell sausage rolls nationwide; supermarket bakery counters also stock equivalent versions.

name: Cornish Pasty with Side Salad
popularityContext: The Cornish pasty is a Protected Geographical Indication British lunch food, originally a miner's portable meal from Cornwall and now sold nationwide in bakeries, supermarkets and Greggs.
prepNotes: Encase diced beef, potato, swede and onion in a pastry parcel, crimp the edge, and bake at 180-200C for around 50 minutes until golden. Serve warm or at room temperature with a side salad.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for pastry, beef and root vegetables; cross-checked against Greggs Cornish pasty nutrition panel (500-560 kcal per pasty)

regionAvailability.notes: A nationwide bakery item with Protected Geographical Indication status; sold fresh and frozen in every major UK supermarket.

name: Chicken Doner Kebab Wrap with Salad
popularityContext: The doner kebab wrap is one of the most popular British takeaway lunches, served from kebab shops on virtually every high street, often as a lighter wrap version rather than the full tray meal.
prepNotes: Slice grilled, marinated chicken from a rotisserie or pan, warm the flatbread, and fill with chicken, shredded salad and a sauce of choice, then fold or roll to serve.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for chicken doner meat, flatbread and salad; cross-checked against chicken doner kebab wrap nutrition data (450-550 kcal per wrap)

name: Vegetable Samosa and Onion Bhaji with Salad
popularityContext: Vegetable samosas and onion bhajis are a popular British Indian takeaway and supermarket deli combination, commonly bought as a light vegetarian lunch or shared starter, including Tesco's well-known fresh food deli pairing.

ingredients.item: mango chutney or mint yoghurt dip

prepNotes: Bake or shallow-fry samosas and bhajis until hot through and crisp, then serve with a side salad and a small pot of mango chutney or mint yoghurt for dipping.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for pastry, potato, vegetables and chutney; cross-checked against Tesco Fresh Food Vegetable Samosa & Onion Bhaji nutrition panel (225 kcal for 2 medium pieces) scaled to a full lunch portion with salad

name: Quiche Lorraine with Coleslaw and Salad
popularityContext: Quiche Lorraine with coleslaw and salad is a classic British café and supermarket deli counter lunch, widely sold by Sainsbury's, M&S and Waitrose cafés as a ready-to-eat plate.
prepNotes: Blind-bake a pastry case, fill with beaten eggs, cream, diced bacon and cheese, then bake at 180C for 30-35 minutes until set and golden. Serve a slice at room temperature with coleslaw and salad.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for pastry, eggs, bacon, cream and cheese; cross-checked against Sainsbury's Cafe quiche lorraine, coleslaw and salad nutrition panel (418 kcal for a lighter portion, scaled up for a standard slice)

prepNotes: Bought ready-made as a mixed selection box of salmon nigiri, salmon and avocado rolls and California rolls, served chilled with soy sauce, pickled ginger and wasabi on the side.

popularityContext: Falafel and hummus wraps have become a mainstream UK lunch option, sold widely in supermarket meal-deal ranges and high-street chains as a popular vegetarian and vegan choice.

ingredients.item: mixed salad leaves, tomato and cucumber

prepNotes: Warm the falafel, spread hummus over the wrap, layer with falafel, salad and a drizzle of tahini or yoghurt dressing, then roll tightly and slice in half.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for chickpea falafel, hummus and wrap bread; consistent with UK supermarket falafel wrap nutrition panels (420-490 kcal)

name: BBQ Chicken, Bacon and Cheese Panini
popularityContext: The BBQ chicken, bacon and cheese panini is a popular hot lunch item at British pub chains and coffee shops such as Wetherspoons and Costa, commonly grabbed as a quick midday meal.
prepNotes: Layer chicken, bacon, cheese and BBQ sauce inside the split panini bread, then press in a panini grill for 3-4 minutes until the bread is toasted and the cheese has melted.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for bread, chicken, bacon and cheese; cross-checked against Wetherspoon BBQ Chicken, Bacon & Cheese Panini nutrition panel (610 kcal, 43g protein)

name: Beans on Toast with Grated Cheese
popularityContext: Beans on toast is one of the most iconic and widely eaten British lunches, valued for being quick, cheap and comforting, and a fixture in homes, school canteens and workplace kitchens nationwide.
prepNotes: Toast the bread, butter while hot, and top with warmed baked beans and a scattering of grated cheddar. Ready in under 10 minutes, making it one of Britain's fastest lunches.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for baked beans, bread, butter and cheddar; consistent with standard beans-on-toast nutrition data (350-460 kcal depending on cheese and bread quantity)

popularityContext: Making a sandwich from leftover Sunday roast chicken is a deeply embedded British Monday-lunch tradition, valued as a practical way to use up roast dinner leftovers.
prepNotes: Layer cold sliced roast chicken, a slice of leftover stuffing and lettuce onto buttered or mayo-spread bread. A practical way to use up Sunday roast leftovers for Monday's lunch.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for roast chicken, bread, stuffing and mayonnaise in a standard sandwich portion

prepNotes: Sold ready-to-eat as a chilled salad box: sliced chicken breast over mixed leaves with tomatoes, cucumber and parmesan, with a separate dressing sachet to add before eating.

prepNotes: Grill bacon until crisp, then layer with lettuce, tomato and mayonnaise between two slices of bread. A quick assembly for a packed lunch or a standard pre-packed meal-deal item.

popularityContext: Couscous salad with chickpeas is a widely made British lunchbox and meal-prep dish, frequently featured in BBC Good Food-style magazine recipes as a make-ahead lunch that travels well.

ingredients.item: cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion

prepNotes: Pour boiling stock over couscous, cover and leave for 5 minutes to absorb, then fluff with a fork. Stir through chickpeas, feta and chopped vegetables, and finish with an olive oil and lemon dressing.

popularityContext: The fish finger sandwich is a beloved British comfort lunch, a nostalgic nod to childhood fish fingers reinvented as a pub and café 'posh' sandwich on menus such as Ember Inns.

ingredients.item: white bloomer or soft bread roll

prepNotes: Grill or oven-bake fish fingers until crisp and golden, then sandwich between buttered bread or a soft roll with lettuce and a generous spoon of tartare sauce.

popularityContext: Pea and ham soup is a traditional British recipe long used to make the most of a leftover gammon or ham joint, commonly cooked at home as a hearty, warming lunch.
prepNotes: Simmer split peas with stock, onion and carrot for 30-40 minutes until soft, then blend until smooth or leave chunky. Stir through diced ham and warm through, then serve with crusty bread.

prepNotes: Lay chicken, bacon, lettuce, parmesan and Caesar dressing along the tortilla, then roll tightly and slice in half. A standard pre-packed format found in supermarket chiller cabinets.

name: Mini Frittata Muffins with Side Salad
popularityContext: Mini frittata muffins are a popular British meal-prep and lunchbox recipe, frequently recommended by egg industry bodies and family food blogs as a portable, protein-rich lunch for adults and children alike.

prepNotes: Whisk eggs with milk, stir through diced vegetables and cheese, then pour into a greased muffin tin and bake at 180C for 18-20 minutes until set. Serve 3-4 muffins with a side salad.

popularityContext: The Melton Mowbray pork pie is a Protected Geographical Indication British food, traditionally eaten cold as a picnic or packed lunch with pickle, and widely sold across UK supermarket deli counters.
prepNotes: Eaten cold, sliced into wedges, with a spoonful of pickle and a side salad. No cooking is required as the pie is fully cooked and typically bought ready to eat.

name: Cottage Pie with Seasonal Vegetables
popularityContext: Cottage pie, made with minced beef and a mashed potato topping, is a deeply traditional British lunch and dinner dish served in homes, school canteens and pub kitchens nationwide.

ingredients.item: potato, mashed with butter and milk

prepNotes: Brown the mince with onion and carrot, simmer in stock and Worcestershire sauce for 20 minutes, then top with mashed potato and bake at 200C for 20-25 minutes until golden. Serve with steamed seasonal vegetables.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for beef mince, potato and vegetables in a standard home-cooked cottage pie portion (500-610 kcal range)

name: Falafel, Houmous and Spiced Carrot Wrap (Meal Deal)
popularityContext: Plant-based falafel and houmous wraps have become a mainstream UK supermarket meal-deal staple, with Tesco's Plant Chef, Morrisons' Plant Revolution and Lidl ranges all selling a near-identical format nationwide.
prepNotes: Sold pre-packed and ready to eat: a tomato tortilla filled with carrot and coriander falafel, houmous, grated carrot, spinach and a touch of mango chutney, rolled and sliced.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for chickpea falafel, houmous and tortilla wrap; cross-checked against Tesco Plant Chef Falafel Houmous Wrap nutrition panel

regionAvailability.notes: Part of widely sold plant-based meal-deal ranges (Tesco Plant Chef, Morrisons Plant Revolution, Co-op GRO, Lidl), available nationwide.

Vegan meal deals for lunch on the go

name: Club Sandwich (Chicken, Bacon, Egg)
popularityContext: The triple-decker club sandwich with chicken, bacon and egg is a popular café and pub lunch order in the UK, commonly served with chips or a side salad in pub chains and independent cafés.
prepNotes: Toast three slices of bread, layer chicken, bacon, egg, lettuce, tomato and mayonnaise between them, then cut into triangular quarters and secure with cocktail sticks, in the classic triple-decker format.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for bread, chicken, bacon, egg and mayonnaise in a standard triple-decker club sandwich portion

regionAvailability.notes: A common pub and café menu item nationwide; pre-packed supermarket club sandwiches are also widely stocked.

sources.title: 'I tried sandwiches from M&S, Greggs, Tesco and Co-op'

name: Lentil Dahl with Brown Rice
popularityContext: Lentil dahl is a widely cooked British-Indian home lunch, popular for being inexpensive, plant-based and high in fibre; it features regularly in NHS healthy-eating recipe guides and BBC Good Food's curry collections.
prepNotes: Fry onion, garlic, ginger and spices until fragrant, add lentils, tomatoes and water, then simmer for 25-30 minutes until the lentils are soft and the dahl has thickened. Serve over brown rice.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for red lentils, brown rice and spices; consistent with NHS and BBC Good Food dahl recipe nutrition data

regionAvailability.notes: Dried lentils, brown rice and curry spices are inexpensive pantry staples sold in every UK supermarket, and this dish is widely cooked at home nationwide.

sources.title: NHS Healthier Families lunchbox and recipe guidance

name: Greek-Style Halloumi and Roasted Vegetable Wrap
popularityContext: Halloumi wraps with roasted Mediterranean vegetables have become a popular UK café and supermarket lunch option as halloumi's mainstream popularity has grown, sold by Pret a Manger and supermarket food-to-go ranges.

ingredients.item: roasted red pepper, courgette and red onion

prepNotes: Grill sliced halloumi until golden, roast the vegetables at 200C for 20 minutes, then spread houmous on the wrap and fill with halloumi, vegetables and salad leaves before rolling.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for halloumi, roasted vegetables, houmous and wrap bread, consistent with Pret a Manger-style halloumi wrap nutrition data

regionAvailability.notes: Sold at Pret a Manger and as a chilled food-to-go item at Waitrose, M&S and Sainsbury's; halloumi is now a mainstream UK supermarket cheese.

sources.title: Pret A Manger Falafel and Halloumi Hot Wrap

name: Tuna Pasta Salad Lunch Pot
popularityContext: Tuna pasta salad is a long-standing British packed-lunch and meal-prep dish, popular for being inexpensive, easy to make in batches and well suited to lunchboxes and office fridges.
prepNotes: Cook and cool the pasta, then mix with flaked tuna, sweetcorn, peas, tomatoes and mayonnaise. Keeps well chilled for 2-3 days, making it a reliable batch-cooked lunch for the working week.

nutrition.method: Calculated from McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods for pasta, tinned tuna, vegetables and mayonnaise in a standard lunchbox-style pasta salad portion

regionAvailability.notes: A budget-friendly, widely made home lunch nationwide; all ingredients are inexpensive pantry and chiller staples in every UK supermarket.

sources.title: 10-Minute Couscous Salad
